title: ROM: Real-time Overthinking Mitigation via Streaming Detection and Intervention
claim_summary: Uses a lightweight hidden-state head for token-level streaming overthinking detection and early answer emission.
assumptions: frozen backbone plus labeled boundaries
ignored_failure_modes: does not define continuation utility or proxy bias
relevance: Strong evidence that hidden states contain online stop information.

title: TERMINATOR: Learning Optimal Exit Points for Early Stopping in Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
claim_summary: Learns first-answer positions and turns them into supervision for optimal reasoning length.
assumptions: optimal first-answer labels are learnable
ignored_failure_modes: does not model repair and corruption hazards explicitly
relevance: Useful operational baseline for answer-arrival prediction.

title: Mitigating Overthinking in Large Reasoning Language Models via Reasoning Path Deviation Monitoring
claim_summary: Stops reasoning when high-entropy transition tokens signal deviation from the correct path.
assumptions: overthinking coincides with deviation events
ignored_failure_modes: weak on utility theory and sequential validity
relevance: Supports entropy and path-deviation as covariates, not as core theory.

title: CODA: Difficulty-Aware Compute Allocation for Adaptive Reasoning
claim_summary: Formalizes adaptive reasoning as utility maximization with marginal gain versus cost.
assumptions: difficulty can be estimated from policy-internal signals
ignored_failure_modes: does not decompose utility into hazards or proxy bias
relevance: Closest literature match to the drift-minus-cost perspective.

title: ODAR: Principled Adaptive Routing for LLM Reasoning via Active Inference
claim_summary: Routes inputs between fast and slow agents using active inference and free-energy fusion.
assumptions: difficulty and epistemic uncertainty guide compute allocation
ignored_failure_modes: stopping is routing-level, not token-level continuation value
relevance: Useful control interpretation and uncertainty signal source.

title: Efficient Reasoning with Balanced Thinking
claim_summary: Steers reasoning away from overthinking and underthinking using confidence variance and hidden-state prototypes.
assumptions: confidence dynamics reflect reasoning mode
ignored_failure_modes: no direct theorem for stopping value
relevance: Supports confidence variance and steering as operational tools.

title: Beyond Outcome Verification: Verifiable Process Reward Models for Structured Reasoning
claim_summary: Replaces neural step judges with deterministic verifiers for intermediate reasoning steps.
assumptions: intermediate steps can be programmatically checked
ignored_failure_modes: domain specificity may limit generality
relevance: Important for exact-step verification and verifier-backed q_t on narrow tasks.

title: Revisiting the (Sub)Optimality of Best-of-N for Inference-Time Alignment
claim_summary: Shows tuned Best-of-N can be win-rate optimal while still remaining reward-hackable.
observables: reward model scores, pairwise win rate
assumptions: win rate is the right objective for some alignment settings
ignored_failure_modes: does not solve continuation stopping inside a single trajectory
relevance: Clarifies that proxy success and proxy misalignment can coexist.

title: The alpha-Law of Observable Belief Revision in Large Language Model Inference
claim_summary: Introduces a revision exponent governing how models combine prior belief and new evidence across iterative inference.
assumptions: observable updates approximate a stable multiplicative law
ignored_failure_modes: does not include compute cost or corruption hazard explicitly
relevance: Useful for modeling q_t dynamics and revision stability.

title: Statistical Early Stopping for Reasoning Models
claim_summary: Uses uncertainty-keyword processes and finite-sample stopping rules to halt reasoning.
assumptions: uncertainty markers align with harmful continuation
ignored_failure_modes: does not identify latent continuation value directly
relevance: Nearest existing stopping paper on formal sequential methods.

title: Stop When Enough: Adaptive Early-Stopping for Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
claim_summary: REFRAIN detects reflective redundancy and tunes thresholds with a sliding-window UCB controller.
assumptions: redundant reasoning can be recognized without supervision
ignored_failure_modes: focuses on threshold tuning more than hazard semantics
relevance: Good practical baseline and evidence that stopping is a control problem.

title: Do LLMs Really Need 10+ Thoughts for Find the Time 1000 Days Later? Towards Structural Understanding of LLM Overthinking
claim_summary: TRACE decomposes long reasoning into sub-thought graphs and identifies over-exploration and over-verification patterns.
assumptions: thought structure is observable enough to analyze
ignored_failure_modes: structural patterns alone do not yield sequential guarantees
relevance: Provides strong descriptive taxonomy for failure modes.

title: Explore Briefly, Then Decide: Mitigating LLM Overthinking via Cumulative Entropy Regulation
claim_summary: Introduces TECA and cumulative entropy regulation to stop when exploration is exhausted.
assumptions: entropy reflects exploration progress
ignored_failure_modes: can confuse uncertainty with useful ongoing repair
relevance: Supports entropy as an informative but incomplete stop signal.

title: Entropy After </Think> for reasoning model early exiting
claim_summary: Uses post-think token entropy variance to stop when Pass@1 has plateaued.
assumptions: entropy stabilizes when accuracy saturates
ignored_failure_modes: needs careful protocol design and may fail on weak models
relevance: Useful lightweight baseline and black-box proxy idea.

title: ThinkPrune: Pruning Long Chain-of-Thought of LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
claim_summary: Uses iterative RL with token limits to shorten reasoning while retaining core steps.
assumptions: training can consolidate reasoning rather than only truncate it
ignored_failure_modes: says little about online stopping on frozen models
relevance: Useful contrast: training-time compression versus inference-time stopping.

title: Trace Length is a Simple Uncertainty Signal in Reasoning Models
claim_summary: Shows trace length itself is a practical uncertainty estimator in reasoning models.
assumptions: post-training changes length-accuracy relation
ignored_failure_modes: length is only a coarse proxy for utility
relevance: Supports uncertainty estimation and trace-length covariates.

title: Understanding and Steering the Cognitive Behaviors of Reasoning Models at Test-Time
claim_summary: Identifies attention heads correlated with verification and backtracking and steers them at inference time.
assumptions: inefficient cognitive modes are locally steerable
ignored_failure_modes: mechanism may be model-specific
relevance: Supports representation-level controls and hidden-state diagnostics.

title: Inference-Time Reward Hacking in Large Language Models
claim_summary: Shows proxy optimization inevitably produces a rise-then-fall true reward pattern unless hedged.
assumptions: proxy misspecification is unavoidable
ignored_failure_modes: does not decompose single-trajectory hazards
relevance: Directly supports the positive-bias term kappa_t and post-boundary overshoot.

title: Robust Reward Modeling via Causal Rubrics
claim_summary: Mitigates reward hacking by separating causal quality attributes from spurious style attributes.
assumptions: causal rubrics can isolate true quality drivers
ignored_failure_modes: depends on access to an oracle LLM for rubric interventions
relevance: Useful for understanding which proxy components create kappa_t.

title: Reward Under Attack: Analyzing the Robustness and Hackability of Process Reward Models
claim_summary: Shows current PRMs often behave like fluency detectors and are highly exploitable under optimization pressure.
observables: PRM scores, attack success, stylistic shortcuts
assumptions: PRM robustness must be stress-tested before use
ignored_failure_modes: does not provide a stopping theorem
relevance: Strongest recent evidence that PRM-only stopping is unsafe.

title: CaRT: Teaching LLM Agents to Know When They Know Enough
claim_summary: Trains termination behavior with counterfactual trajectory pairs where stopping is and is not appropriate.
assumptions: counterfactual pairs can teach stop-versus-continue decisions
ignored_failure_modes: focuses on interactive agents more than token-level reasoning traces
relevance: Useful for value-of-computation and intervention framing.

title: Conformal Thinking: Risk Control for Reasoning on a Compute Budget
claim_summary: Turns compute-budget selection into distribution-free risk control with upper and lower stopping thresholds.
assumptions: validation data can calibrate stopping thresholds
ignored_failure_modes: distribution-free guarantees still rely on calibration stability
relevance: Important bridge from stopping heuristics to explicit risk guarantees.

title: E-values for Adaptive Clinical Trials: Anytime-Valid Monitoring in Practice
claim_summary: Explains e-values and e-processes for optional stopping with continuous monitoring.
assumptions: anytime-valid betting constructions remain calibrated under optional stopping
ignored_failure_modes: domain is clinical trials, not LLMs
relevance: Best external reference for building stronger sequential guarantees.

title: Game-theoretic statistics and safe anytime-valid inference
claim_summary: Surveys e-processes and confidence sequences as the right objects for safe anytime-valid inference.
assumptions: nonnegative martingales are the correct evidence objects under optional stopping
ignored_failure_modes: not specific to LLMs or reasoning traces
relevance: Foundational justification for sequential validity claims in this repo.

title: No Global Plan in Chain-of-Thought: Uncover the Latent Planning Horizon of LLMs
claim_summary: Probes hidden states and argues LLM reasoning is myopic, with only short latent planning horizons.
assumptions: small subsets of CoT positions summarize path uncertainty
ignored_failure_modes: does not directly address stopping cost tradeoffs
relevance: Supports myopic hazard models over strong global-plan assumptions.

title: Calibrating LLM Judges: Linear Probes for Fast and Reliable Uncertainty Estimation
claim_summary: Uses linear probes on hidden states to produce calibrated uncertainty estimates much faster than multi-sample methods.
assumptions: hidden states encode judge uncertainty in a linearly accessible way
ignored_failure_modes: current estimates can be conservative on easy data
relevance: Relevant for q_t estimation from hidden states.

title: DiffAdapt: Difficulty-Adaptive Reasoning for Token-Efficient LLM Inference
claim_summary: Uses entropy and a small final-state probe to select easy, normal, or hard inference strategies.
assumptions: difficulty is classifiable from lightweight features
ignored_failure_modes: relies on strategy bins rather than direct continuation value
relevance: Strong evidence that entropy plus hidden-state probes are actionable.

title: The Virtues of Brevity: Avoid Overthinking in Parallel Test-Time Reasoning
claim_summary: Finds that shortest-answer selection often beats more elaborate parallel selectors because verbose samples come from an uncertain overthinking regime.
assumptions: short answers preferentially sample the concise regime
ignored_failure_modes: parallel selection is not single-trajectory stopping
relevance: Supports a two-regime view and brevity-sensitive anti-baseline.

title: Learning to Ponder: Adaptive Reasoning in Latent Space
claim_summary: Uses a tiny controller and latent steering vector to allocate extra pondering steps only where useful.
assumptions: latent-space control can replace explicit long CoT
ignored_failure_modes: does not expose repair and corruption hazards explicitly
relevance: Useful evidence that reasoning depth can be controlled in representation space.

title: Skip a Layer or Loop it? Test-Time Depth Adaptation of Pretrained LLMs
claim_summary: Shows that per-sample layer skipping and looping can improve both efficiency and accuracy without finetuning.
assumptions: depth can be adapted per sample at test time
ignored_failure_modes: architecture-level adaptation is more general than token-level stopping
relevance: Supports the broader compute-allocation framing beyond token count alone.
